statement_refuted: NEW KEY LEMMA CANDIDATE E_3 <= ell (experimental/notes/l1/l1_prime_ell_key_lemma_refuted.md Sec 5)
Residual Conjecture RC: T >= 5 => E_3 <= ell (experimental/notes/l1/l1_sigma_calculus.md Sec 2A.2)
verdict: COUNTEREXAMPLE: max observed E_3 = ell + 2, all six witnesses on the T>=5 (residual) chart; Theorem 1 (T<=4 => E_3<=ell), the pairwise cap, and the master identity all SURVIVE on the counterexamples

witnesses.note: STRONGEST witness: E_3 = ell+2. Structure = one size-(ell-3) fiber + seven size-3 fibers across all 8 cosets (all cosets active, n=K=8).

shallow_coverage_table.description: Shallow (1-17 planted-pair attempts per prime) randomized under-plant-then-sweep sweeps at ell in {13,19,31}, testing whether the boundary E_3=ell is reached (without necessarily crossing it into a violation E_3>=ell+1). Source: lane-B2 session logs (focused_extent.py runs, tags A-D).

rows.note: boundary hit confirmed only by a DEEPER rerun (pair_attempts=6; an earlier pair_attempts=4 run at this same prime found only max_E3=18); see provenance_discrepancy below.
no deeper rerun of this prime exists in the lane-B2 artifacts; see provenance_discrepancy below.

shallow_coverage_table.provenance_discrepancy: The note Sec 0.5 attributes the ell=19 boundary hit to p=191 only (found by a deeper rerun, pair_attempts=6, superseding an earlier pair_attempts=4 run that reached 18); the shallow p=229 pass reached only ell-1=18 in every recorded run. This table matches that corrected attribution. Parallel hedge applies at ell=13: boundary hit at p in {53,79} only; p in {131,157} reached ell-1.

method_provenance.general: The winning method (per the note Sec 0 item 3): UNDER-plant a big-fiber pair (mu1+mu2 = ell-1, nullspace dim 2, a pencil; or ell-2 seeded on a pre-existing object's top-2 fibers, dim 3) and EXHAUSTIVELY sweep the entire resulting projective nullspace family, reading each member's EMERGENT spectrum. Contrast: the integrated law-evidence and census constructors read only one nullspace vector (basis[0]) or random-sample the family with probability ~1/p per config -- both systematically miss the extremal members.
